Is AI Getting Quietly Dumber? AIStupidLevel: A 24-Hour Watchdog for LLM Degradation

This post walks through AIStupidLevel — a third-party benchmark site that keeps a 24-hour eye on 21 production AI models across 7 providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM). It throws coding, reasoning, and tool-calling tasks at them every few hours, runs each task 5 times to take the median with a 95% confidence interval, and uses the CUSUM change-point detection algorithm to catch when a model is quietly getting dumber. We'll cover why models degrade (silent updates, quantization), how the four test suites work, how to read the Stupid Meter, and the bundled Smart Router that auto-picks the best model for you.
How to Use GPT-5.5 and ChatGPT Images 2.0: 30 Prompt Templates for IG Cards, Knowledge Decks, and Product Mockups

OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 and ChatGPT Images 2.0 in the same week of late April 2026. GPT-5.5 is the new language model that handles understanding and reasoning; ChatGPT Images 2.0 (codename gpt-image-2) is the dedicated image generation model. The leap in image generation this round comes from those two working together — text rendering finally doesn't blur out, and Chinese, English, and Japanese all come out correct. This post clarifies how the two models split the work, then walks through what's new in GPT-5.5, what makes ChatGPT Images 2.0 strong, and ends with 30 copy-paste prompt templates across IG cards, knowledge decks, carousel covers, blog headers, infographics, product mockups, quote cards, and itineraries.
Build Your Own AI Language Model Lab — No Help Needed, with LM Studio

Most of us have been bombarded by AI, ChatGPT, and other large language models for a while now, and we've all been using web services like ChatGPT or Claude.AI. The upside of these services is that they're easy to start with and convenient. The downsides? You need an internet connection, there are data privacy concerns, and there are API costs. So is there a way to self-host AI locally? Yep — and this post introduces a really handy tool for that called LM Studio.
Use ChatGPT Without Paying or Writing Code (2023 Snapshot)

Heads up — this post is from April 2023, written when ChatGPT was newly available and people weren't sure how to register. The signup flow described here has changed since (it's much simpler now), and the noted "data cutoff is 2021" is also outdated as newer models have launched. The prompting tips section still applies. Worth keeping as a snapshot of early ChatGPT adoption.

Build Your First LINE Bot with Node.js — Using OpenAI GPT-3 and GPT-3.5 as Examples

OpenAI's ChatGPT has been blowing up recently, and OpenAI also exposes an API we can integrate with. This post focuses on GPT-3 but also covers how to switch to ChatGPT API. We'll use Node.js to build a LINE chatbot backed by OpenAI.
